title: Why Could Toji See Shinjis Dream
display_title: Why Could Toji See Shinjis Dream
wikitext:
[]: Why Could Toji See Shinji's Dream?             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:27 pm I don't remember this being discussed before, and even I didn't think about it until now (except while watching the episode, immediately forgetting about it when the scene was over) In episode 19, when Toji is half-conscious and next to Shinji in the hospital, he appears to be viewing a dream that Shinji is having inside his "mind-train", in which he is having an argument with Rei about Gendo. Now, why could Toji see into Shinji's mind? Instrumentality hadn't started yet!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:42 pm there was a short thread over at ANF about this: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=183255 personally, I'm not sure wtf is going on. The best thing I can think of is Rei inadvertantly connecting Toji to Shinji's dream.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:43 pm Quantum magic.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:52 pm MaggotMaster wrote: Quantum magic.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sat Dec 23, 2006 11:58 pm NAveryW wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Quantum magic.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:09 am MaggotMaster wrote: NAveryW wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: Quantum magic.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:31 am Ornette wrote: there was a short thread over at ANF about this: http://www.animenation.net/forums/showthread.php?t=183255 personally, I'm not sure wtf is going on. The best thing I can think of is Rei inadvertantly connecting Toji to Shinji's dream.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Dec 24, 2006 2:47 am I forgot to make a disclaimer: Fuzzy Chickens is reknown for not making sense

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 3:35 am Ornette wrote: I forgot to make a disclaimer: Fuzzy Chickens is reknown for not making sense

[]:             Quote Post by sir_evangelist » Sun Dec 24, 2006 10:01 am i think it tries to explain that humans are separated and lonely. Touji coulnÂ´t hear what they where talking about and he was in another part of the train.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 24, 2006 10:07 am Listen to the way Toji is talking. He's drugged. He's lucid. This is nothing more than a simple hallucination capitalizing on the recurring train motif.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 24, 2006 12:46 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Listen to the way Toji is talking. He's drugged. He's lucid. This is nothing more than a simple hallucination capitalizing on the recurring train motif.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 24, 2006 4:51 pm Fuzzy Chicken makes some sense with this theory despite the fact that in most of his posts I've read he behaves like a stupid troll.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Dec 24, 2006 5:55 pm I think the moment I watched that scene, I wondered wtf was going on with that. I guess it must be Lilith's Quantam Magic at work, though I wouldn't know the reasoning behind it. *shrug* Agreed with NAveryW as well as what Eva Monkey said; haven't read Fuzzy Troll's post so I can't give my thoughts on it. EDIT: Shame that he couldn't make more sense like this more of the time. :roll: In terms of the train motif, besides 19 & 21'(? 20?), there's also: ~episode 4 Shinji, in acutal train listening to his SDAT ~22' Asuka, at station seeing Rei & Shinji (I think it's a parallel to that scene from episode 5: Shinji watching Rei & Gendou talking, not sure though.) ~22' mindrape when Asuka sees those Rei(?? Asuka(?)) clones attack, begging for Kaji-san's help. ~26' P3II scene "Be kind to me." Misato/Rei/Asuka: "I am kind to you." *whew*

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Dec 25, 2006 5:05 pm NAveryW wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Listen to the way Toji is talking. He's drugged. He's lucid. This is nothing more than a simple hallucination capitalizing on the recurring train motif.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Dec 25, 2006 5:27 pm W... what? Do you mean to say that Rei was in the room with Shinji, but Toji, half-asleep and on drugs, saw it taking place on the train?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Dec 25, 2006 9:44 pm NAveryW wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Listen to the way Toji is talking. He's drugged. He's lucid. This is nothing more than a simple hallucination capitalizing on the recurring train motif.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Dec 25, 2006 9:54 pm Sachiel_13 wrote: NAveryW wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Listen to the way Toji is talking. He's drugged. He's lucid. This is nothing more than a simple hallucination capitalizing on the recurring train motif.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Tue Dec 26, 2006 2:32 am Toji is actually a psychic, he was going to get selected for the United Powers League Ghost program.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 26, 2006 6:16 pm NAveryW wrote: W... what? Do you mean to say that Rei was in the room with Shinji, but Toji, half-asleep and on drugs, saw it taking place on the train?

Now, why could Toji see into Shinji's mind? Instrumentality hadn't started yet!
